mission.title: strimzi: Fix renewal of user provided CAs
mission.description:
This PR fixes #5466.
Today, when a user provides his own CA certificate (cluster or client) but then he tries to follow the [renew process](https://strim

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description:
This PR fixes #5466.
Today, when a user provides his own CA certificate (cluster or client) but then he tries to follow the [renew process](https://strim

steps.title: Apply the configuration
steps.description:
Apply the following configuration to your cluster:
```yaml
annotations:
    clients-ca-thumbprint: hCyUdeqWt6gXDUKYdAtaFYDiPoZlWAwCjW7liqtWkps=
```

steps.title: Review the fix
steps.description: The fix was implemented in https://github.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/pull/6240. Review the changes to understand the solution.

steps.title: Verify the fix
steps.description: Confirm that the issue is resolved in your environment by testing the affected functionality.

resolution.summary:
This is the documentation related update for PR #6180 which fixes issues #5466.
The PR adds more information about certification generations to be added to the Secret for handling your own CA certificates and corresponding renewal.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with strimzi installed or the issue environment reproducible.
